Ex Parte Petition To Advance Hearing Date On Petition To Change Jurisdiction And Transfer Proceedings To Placer County, California To Same Date As Transfer Hearing In Companion Conservatorship Case On October 28, 2020
Off Calendar Sep-28-2020 Denied

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jul-14-2020 Vacated

Petition Of Respondent Brent J. Long For The Courts Approval Of Attorney Fees Under The Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jul-17-2020 Text Ruling

Petition For Financial Abuse Of An Elder, To Recover Assets Wrongfully Transferred From Trust, For Breach Of Constructive Trust, For Accounting, And F
Off Calendar Jul-14-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Konstantine Demiris appeared by Zoom for and with David Long (Co-Trustee) by Zoom, Patricia Bishop (Co-Trustee) by Zoom and Brian Long (Co-Conservator) by Zoom.
Ray Erlach appeared by Zoom for Brent Long.

Mr. Demiris stated that there is the issue of whether to supervise the trust, or not. The Court noted that the issue of court supervision is not on calendar. Mr. Erlach stated that the settlement agreement provides that Brent could request Court approval for fees over $50,000.00, that everything that was done was a benefit to the estate and that this is a highly contentious matter that got settled. Mr. Erlach also stated that Patricia and Brent could not serve together, that there was no way to settle ties or disputes between them, that Brent Long has resigned, that the trust will remain in place as long Angela is alive and that this was a huge benefit. The Court noted that the hours spent were more on the elder abuse petition, rather than the trust. Mr. Erlach stated that every task is laid out and that there are no block billing entries. Mr. Demiris stated that California Rules of Court 7.702 and Local Rule 14.86 generally governs petitions for fees, that the settlement agreement allowed for petitioning for fees, but it did not consent to the petition or consent to the fees, and that there is no legal authority that allows a Respondent in an elder abuse claim to seek Attorney's fees in a trust. The Court noted that there is a consensus that there was a benefit to the trust. Mr. Demiris stated that he believes the status hearing is moot, that the sale of the house has been cancelled, that the house is not being sold and that change of venue is before the Court. The Court noted that normally it would order that the trust be brought under Court supervision, that venue is now before the Court and that it will defer to Placer County as to how they want to address the twin issues. The Parties have no objection to the Petition for Financial Abuse of An Elder, etc. going off calendar. Said petition of off calendar. The status hearing is vacated. The Court will not take the trust under Court supervision at this time. The Petition of Brent Long for Court Approval of Attorney's Fees Under the Settlement Agreement is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Feb-10-2020 Continued To Apr-06-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jan-27-2020 Continued To Mar-16-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Konstantine Demiris and Christopher Moore for and with Patty Bishop and for and with David Long (appeared by telephone) (Co-Trustees).
Ernest Der for and with Debra Dolch (Temporary Conservator).
Raymond Erlach appeared by telephone for and with Brent Long (present in Court).
Charles Waite appeared by telephone.

Mr. Der addressed Ms. Waite's change in residence stating that they thought it was going to be a temporary placement, that if she is doing well, it is in her best interest and that his position is that this is the best placement for her. Mr. Demiris stated that he believes the declaration from the Doctor is substantial evidence to support that it is in the best interest of the Conservatee to remain where she is, read the declaration from a Doctor, spoke of Ms. Waite's medical condition, stated that his Clients are not hiding anything, that the Conservator was informed in late October of the ability to have a facility in Rockland, that the settlement agreement was noticed and approved, that provisions were being made, that the Conservatee is receiving 24/7 care, etc. Ms. Dolch stated that prior to the move she made visits once daily, that she was communicating with Karen Long, that they were trying to make accommodations for Angela so that she could be closer to her Sons and that Dennis Mesina was told. Ms. Dolch also that since the move she has had very little communication with Angela, that her communication was primarily with the Villa, Dr. Morris and Karen, and that the hope was that the Sons would petition for appointment, but they didn't. The Court stated that even when someone lacks capacity to make medical decisions, it does not mean that they don't have capacity to show a desire to be somewhere, or not to be somewhere, raised the issue of the need to have Mr. Mesina here and possibly Ms. Waite, and stated that there are still a number of questions. Mr. Demiris stated that post move notice was provided and that no one is objecting. The Court noted that the probate code wasn't followed, and that notice was provided because of the Court Investigator. The Court stated that the Court order of January 17, 2020 will remain in place and there is to be no sale of real property until further of the Court. The Court also stated that now that the Conservator and the Trustee are not the same person, it has concerns. The status hearing is continued to March 16, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The conservatorship matters on calendar for February 13, 2020 are continued to March 12, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Der's petition for change of residence is to be given a hearing date of March 12, 2020. Mr. Der is to advise Mr. Mesina.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Anasse. Not reported.

Off Calendar Nov-08-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Konstantine Demiris, Counsel for Douglas Long, David Long, and Brian Long; Maria Lawless, counsel for Petitioners David Waite and Patricia Bishop; Ernest Der, counsel for Trustee Debra Dolch; Ray Erlach, counsel for Respondent, Brent Long.

At the Court's inquiry, counsel confirm that their clients are all in agreement with the terms of the settlement and further represent that there are no objectors to the best of their knowledge. The Court reports that it will further review the proposed Order but grants the Petition approving the settlement.

Judge: Hon. Peter J. Busch; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)
